2293 FileAddressToAddressClassMap address_class_map;
2294
2295 static ConstString text_section_name(".text");
2296 static ConstString init_section_name(".init");
2297 static ConstString fini_section_name(".fini");
2298 static ConstString ctors_section_name(".ctors");
2299 static ConstString dtors_section_name(".dtors");
2300
2301 static ConstString data_section_name(".data");
2302 static ConstString rodata_section_name(".rodata");
2303 static ConstString rodata1_section_name(".rodata1");
2304 static ConstString data2_section_name(".data1");
2305 static ConstString bss_section_name(".bss");
2306 static ConstString opd_section_name(".opd"); // For ppc64
2307
2308 // On Android the oatdata and the oatexec symbols in the oat and odex files
2309 // covers the full .text section what causes issues with displaying unusable
2310 // symbol name to the user and very slow unwinding speed because the
2311 // instruction emulation based unwind plans try to emulate all instructions
2312 // in these symbols. Don't add these symbols to the symbol list as they have
2313 // no use for the debugger and they are causing a lot of trouble. Filtering
2314 // can't be restricted to Android because this special object file don't
2315 // contain the note section specifying the environment to Android but the
2316 // custom extension and file name makes it highly unlikely that this will
2317 // collide with anything else.
2318 llvm::StringRef file_extension = m_file.GetFileNameExtension();
2319 bool skip_oatdata_oatexec =
2320 file_extension == ".oat" || file_extension == ".odex";
2321
2322 ArchSpec arch = GetArchitecture();
2323 ModuleSP module_sp(GetModule());
2324 SectionList *module_section_list =
2325 module_sp ? module_sp->GetSectionList() : nullptr;
2326
2327 // We might have debug information in a separate object, in which case
2328 // we need to map the sections from that object to the sections in the
2329 // main object during symbol lookup. If we had to compare the sections
2330 // for every single symbol, that would be expensive, so this map is
2331 // used to accelerate the process.
2332 std::unordered_map<lldb::SectionSP, lldb::SectionSP> section_map;
2333
2334 unsigned i;
2335 for (i = 0; i < num_symbols; ++i) {
2336 if (!symbol.Parse(symtab_data, &offset))
2337 break;
2338
2339 const char *symbol_name = strtab_data.PeekCStr(symbol.st_name);
2340 if (!symbol_name)
2341 symbol_name = "";
2342
2343 // Skip local symbols starting with ".L" because these are compiler
2344 // generated local labels used for internal purposes (e.g. debugging,
2345 // optimization) and are not relevant for symbol resolution or external
2346 // linkage.
2347 if (llvm::StringRef(symbol_name).starts_with(".L"))
2348 continue;
2349 // No need to add non-section symbols that have no names
2350 if (symbol.getType() != STT_SECTION &&
2351 (symbol_name == nullptr || symbol_name[0] == '\0'))
2352 continue;
2353
2354 // Skipping oatdata and oatexec sections if it is requested. See details
2355 // above the definition of skip_oatdata_oatexec for the reasons.
2356 if (skip_oatdata_oatexec && (::strcmp(symbol_name, "oatdata") == 0 ||
2357 ::strcmp(symbol_name, "oatexec") == 0))
2358 continue;
2359
2360 SectionSP symbol_section_sp;
2361 SymbolType symbol_type = eSymbolTypeInvalid;
2362 Elf64_Half shndx = symbol.st_shndx;
2363
2364 switch (shndx) {
2365 case SHN_ABS:
2366 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeAbsolute;
2367 break;
2368 case SHN_UNDEF:
2369 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeUndefined;
2370 break;
2371 default:
2372 symbol_section_sp = section_list->FindSectionByID(shndx);
2373 break;
2374 }
2375
2376 // If a symbol is undefined do not process it further even if it has a STT
2377 // type
2378 if (symbol_type != eSymbolTypeUndefined) {
2379 switch (symbol.getType()) {
2380 default:
2381 case STT_NOTYPE:
2382 // The symbol's type is not specified.
2383 break;
2384
2385 case STT_OBJECT:
2386 // The symbol is associated with a data object, such as a variable, an
2387 // array, etc.
2388 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeData;
2389 break;
2390
2391 case STT_FUNC:
2392 // The symbol is associated with a function or other executable code.
2393 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeCode;
2394 break;
2395
2396 case STT_SECTION:
2397 // The symbol is associated with a section. Symbol table entries of
2398 // this type exist primarily for relocation and normally have STB_LOCAL
2399 // binding.
2400 break;
2401
2402 case STT_FILE:
2403 // Conventionally, the symbol's name gives the name of the source file
2404 // associated with the object file. A file symbol has STB_LOCAL
2405 // binding, its section index is SHN_ABS, and it precedes the other
2406 // STB_LOCAL symbols for the file, if it is present.
2407 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeSourceFile;
2408 break;
2409
2410 case STT_GNU_IFUNC:
2411 // The symbol is associated with an indirect function. The actual
2412 // function will be resolved if it is referenced.
2413 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeResolver;
2414 break;
2415
2416 case STT_TLS:
2417 // The symbol is associated with a thread-local data object, such as
2418 // a thread-local variable.
2419 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeData;
2420 break;
2421 }
2422 }
2423
2424 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeInvalid && symbol.getType() != STT_SECTION) {
2425 if (symbol_section_sp) {
2426 ConstString sect_name = symbol_section_sp->GetName();
2427 if (sect_name == text_section_name || sect_name == init_section_name ||
2428 sect_name == fini_section_name || sect_name == ctors_section_name ||
2429 sect_name == dtors_section_name) {
2430 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeCode;
2431 } else if (sect_name == data_section_name ||
2432 sect_name == data2_section_name ||
2433 sect_name == rodata_section_name ||
2434 sect_name == rodata1_section_name ||
2435 sect_name == bss_section_name) {
2436 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeData;
2437 }
2438 }
2439 }
2440
2441 int64_t symbol_value_offset = 0;
2442 uint32_t additional_flags = 0;
2443 if (arch.IsValid()) {
2444 if (arch.GetMachine() == llvm::Triple::arm) {
2445 if (symbol.getBinding() == STB_LOCAL) {
2446 char mapping_symbol = FindArmAarch64MappingSymbol(symbol_name);
2447 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode) {
2448 switch (mapping_symbol) {
2449 case 'a':
2450 // $a[.<any>]* - marks an ARM instruction sequence
2451 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCode;
2452 break;
2453 case 'b':
2454 case 't':
2455 // $b[.<any>]* - marks a THUMB BL instruction sequence
2456 // $t[.<any>]* - marks a THUMB instruction sequence
2457 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] =
2458 AddressClass::eCodeAlternateISA;
2459 break;
2460 case 'd':
2461 // $d[.<any>]* - marks a data item sequence (e.g. lit pool)
2462 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eData;
2463 break;
2464 }
2465 }
2466 if (mapping_symbol)
2467 continue;
2468 }
2469 } else if (arch.GetMachine() == llvm::Triple::aarch64) {
2470 if (symbol.getBinding() == STB_LOCAL) {
2471 char mapping_symbol = FindArmAarch64MappingSymbol(symbol_name);
2472 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode) {
2473 switch (mapping_symbol) {
2474 case 'x':
2475 // $x[.<any>]* - marks an A64 instruction sequence
2476 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCode;
2477 break;
2478 case 'd':
2479 // $d[.<any>]* - marks a data item sequence (e.g. lit pool)
2480 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eData;
2481 break;
2482 }
2483 }
2484 if (mapping_symbol)
2485 continue;
2486 }
2487 } else if (arch.GetTriple().isRISCV()) {
2488 if (symbol.getBinding() == STB_LOCAL) {
2489 char mapping_symbol = FindRISCVMappingSymbol(symbol_name);
2490 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode) {
2491 // Only handle $d and $x mapping symbols.
2492 // Other mapping symbols are ignored as they don't affect address
2493 // classification.
2494 switch (mapping_symbol) {
2495 case 'x':
2496 // $x - marks a RISCV instruction sequence
2497 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCode;
2498 break;
2499 case 'd':
2500 // $d - marks a RISCV data item sequence
2501 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eData;
2502 break;
2503 }
2504 }
2505 if (mapping_symbol)
2506 continue;
2507 }
2508 }
2509
2510 if (arch.GetMachine() == llvm::Triple::arm) {
2511 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode) {
2512 if (symbol.st_value & 1) {
2513 // Subtracting 1 from the address effectively unsets the low order
2514 // bit, which results in the address actually pointing to the
2515 // beginning of the symbol. This delta will be used below in
2516 // conjunction with symbol.st_value to produce the final
2517 // symbol_value that we store in the symtab.
2518 symbol_value_offset = -1;
2519 address_class_map[symbol.st_value ^ 1] =
2520 AddressClass::eCodeAlternateISA;
2521 } else {
2522 // This address is ARM
2523 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCode;
2524 }
2525 }
2526 }
2527
2528 /*
2529 * MIPS:
2530 * The bit #0 of an address is used for ISA mode (1 for microMIPS, 0 for
2531 * MIPS).
2532 * This allows processor to switch between microMIPS and MIPS without any
2533 * need
2534 * for special mode-control register. However, apart from .debug_line,
2535 * none of
2536 * the ELF/DWARF sections set the ISA bit (for symbol or section). Use
2537 * st_other
2538 * flag to check whether the symbol is microMIPS and then set the address
2539 * class
2540 * accordingly.
2541 */
2542 if (arch.IsMIPS()) {
2543 if (IS_MICROMIPS(symbol.st_other))
2544 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCodeAlternateISA;
2545 else if ((symbol.st_value & 1) && (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode)) {
2546 symbol.st_value = symbol.st_value & (~1ull);
2547 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCodeAlternateISA;
2548 } else {
2549 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode)
2550 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eCode;
2551 else if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeData)
2552 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eData;
2553 else
2554 address_class_map[symbol.st_value] = AddressClass::eUnknown;
2555 }
2556 }
2557 }
2558
2559 // symbol_value_offset may contain 0 for ARM symbols or -1 for THUMB
2560 // symbols. See above for more details.
2561 uint64_t symbol_value = symbol.st_value + symbol_value_offset;
2562
2563 if (symbol_section_sp &&
2564 CalculateType() != ObjectFile::Type::eTypeObjectFile)
2565 symbol_value -= symbol_section_sp->GetFileAddress();
2566
2567 if (symbol_section_sp && module_section_list &&
2568 module_section_list != section_list) {
2569 auto section_it = section_map.find(symbol_section_sp);
2570 if (section_it == section_map.end()) {
2571 section_it = section_map
2572 .emplace(symbol_section_sp,
2573 FindMatchingSection(*module_section_list,
2574 symbol_section_sp))
2575 .first;
2576 }
2577 if (section_it->second)
2578 symbol_section_sp = section_it->second;
2579 }
2580
2581 bool is_global = symbol.getBinding() == STB_GLOBAL;
2582 uint32_t flags = symbol.st_other << 8 | symbol.st_info | additional_flags;
2583 llvm::StringRef symbol_ref(symbol_name);
2584
2585 // Symbol names may contain @VERSION suffixes. Find those and strip them
2586 // temporarily.
2587 size_t version_pos = symbol_ref.find('@');
2588 bool has_suffix = version_pos != llvm::StringRef::npos;
2589 llvm::StringRef symbol_bare = symbol_ref.substr(0, version_pos);
2590 Mangled mangled(symbol_bare);
2591
2592 // Now append the suffix back to mangled and unmangled names. Only do it if
2593 // the demangling was successful (string is not empty).
2594 if (has_suffix) {
2595 llvm::StringRef suffix = symbol_ref.substr(version_pos);
2596
2597 llvm::StringRef mangled_name = mangled.GetMangledName().GetStringRef();
2598 if (!mangled_name.empty())
2599 mangled.SetMangledName(ConstString((mangled_name + suffix).str()));
2600
2601 ConstString demangled = mangled.GetDemangledName();
2602 llvm::StringRef demangled_name = demangled.GetStringRef();
2603 if (!demangled_name.empty())
2604 mangled.SetDemangledName(ConstString((demangled_name + suffix).str()));
2605 }
2606
2607 // In ELF all symbol should have a valid size but it is not true for some
2608 // function symbols coming from hand written assembly. As none of the
2609 // function symbol should have 0 size we try to calculate the size for
2610 // these symbols in the symtab with saying that their original size is not
2611 // valid.
2612 bool symbol_size_valid =
2613 symbol.st_size != 0 || symbol.getType() != STT_FUNC;
2614
2615 bool is_trampoline = false;
2616 if (arch.IsValid() && (arch.GetMachine() == llvm::Triple::aarch64)) {
2617 // On AArch64, trampolines are registered as code.
2618 // If we detect a trampoline (which starts with __AArch64ADRPThunk_ or
2619 // __AArch64AbsLongThunk_) we register the symbol as a trampoline. This
2620 // way we will be able to detect the trampoline when we step in a function
2621 // and step through the trampoline.
2622 if (symbol_type == eSymbolTypeCode) {
2623 llvm::StringRef trampoline_name = mangled.GetName().GetStringRef();
2624 if (trampoline_name.starts_with("__AArch64ADRPThunk_") ||
2625 trampoline_name.starts_with("__AArch64AbsLongThunk_")) {
2626 symbol_type = eSymbolTypeTrampoline;
2627 is_trampoline = true;
2628 }
2629 }
2630 }
2631
2632 Symbol dc_symbol(
2633 i + start_id, // ID is the original symbol table index.
2634 mangled,
2635 symbol_type, // Type of this symbol
2636 is_global, // Is this globally visible?
2637 false, // Is this symbol debug info?
2638 is_trampoline, // Is this symbol a trampoline?
2639 false, // Is this symbol artificial?
2640 AddressRange(symbol_section_sp, // Section in which this symbol is
2641 // defined or null.
2642 symbol_value, // Offset in section or symbol value.
2643 symbol.st_size), // Size in bytes of this symbol.
2644 symbol_size_valid, // Symbol size is valid
2645 has_suffix, // Contains linker annotations?
2646 flags); // Symbol flags.
2647 if (symbol.getBinding() == STB_WEAK)
2648 dc_symbol.SetIsWeak(true);
2649 symtab->AddSymbol(dc_symbol);
2650 }
2651
2652 m_address_class_map.merge(address_class_map);
2653 return {i, address_class_map};
2654}
2655
2656std::pair<unsigned, ObjectFileELF::FileAddressToAddressClassMap>
2657ObjectFileELF::ParseSymbolTable(Symtab *symbol_table, user_id_t start_id,
2658 lldb_private::Section *symtab) {
2659 if (symtab->GetObjectFile() != this) {
2660 // If the symbol table section is owned by a different object file, have it
2661 // do the parsing.
2662 ObjectFileELF *obj_file_elf =
2663 static_cast<ObjectFileELF *>(symtab->GetObjectFile());
2664 auto [num_symbols, address_class_map] =
2665 obj_file_elf->ParseSymbolTable(symbol_table, start_id, symtab);
2666
2667 // The other object file returned the changes it made to its address
2668 // class map, make the same changes to ours.
2669 m_address_class_map.merge(address_class_map);
2670
2671 return {num_symbols, address_class_map};
2672 }
2673
2674 // Get section list for this object file.
2675 SectionList *section_list = m_sections_up.get();
2676 if (!section_list)
2677 return {};
2678
2679 user_id_t symtab_id = symtab->GetID();
2680 const ELFSectionHeaderInfo *symtab_hdr = GetSectionHeaderByIndex(symtab_id);
2681 assert(symtab_hdr->sh_type == SHT_SYMTAB ||
2682 symtab_hdr->sh_type == SHT_DYNSYM);
2683
2684 // sh_link: section header index of associated string table.
2685 user_id_t strtab_id = symtab_hdr->sh_link;
2686 Section *strtab = section_list->FindSectionByID(strtab_id).get();
2687
2688 if (symtab && strtab) {
2689 assert(symtab->GetObjectFile() == this);
2690 assert(strtab->GetObjectFile() == this);
2691
2692 DataExtractor symtab_data;
2693 DataExtractor strtab_data;
2694 if (ReadSectionData(symtab, symtab_data) &&
2695 ReadSectionData(strtab, strtab_data)) {
2696 size_t num_symbols = symtab_data.GetByteSize() / symtab_hdr->sh_entsize;
2697
2698 return ParseSymbols(symbol_table, start_id, section_list, num_symbols,
2699 symtab_data, strtab_data);
2700 }
2701 }
2702
2703 return {0, {}};
2704}
2705
2706size_t ObjectFileELF::ParseDynamicSymbols() {
2707 if (m_dynamic_symbols.size())
2708 return m_dynamic_symbols.size();
2709
2710 std::optional<DataExtractor> dynamic_data = GetDynamicData();
2711 if (!dynamic_data)
2712 return 0;
2713
2714 ELFDynamicWithName e;
2715 lldb::offset_t cursor = 0;
2716 while (e.symbol.Parse(*dynamic_data, &cursor)) {
2717 m_dynamic_symbols.push_back(e);
2718 if (e.symbol.d_tag == DT_NULL)
2719 break;
2720 }
2721 if (std::optional<DataExtractor> dynstr_data = GetDynstrData()) {
2722 for (ELFDynamicWithName &entry : m_dynamic_symbols) {
2723 switch (entry.symbol.d_tag) {
2724 case DT_NEEDED:
2725 case DT_SONAME:
2726 case DT_RPATH:
2727 case DT_RUNPATH:
2728 case DT_AUXILIARY:
2729 case DT_FILTER: {
2730 lldb::offset_t cursor = entry.symbol.d_val;
2731 const char *name = dynstr_data->GetCStr(&cursor);
2732 if (name)
2733 entry.name = std::string(name);
2734 break;
2735 }
2736 default:
2737 break;
2738 }
2739 }
2740 }
2741 return m_dynamic_symbols.size();
2742}
2743
2744const ELFDynamic *ObjectFileELF::FindDynamicSymbol(unsigned tag) {
2745 if (!ParseDynamicSymbols())
2746 return nullptr;
2747 for (const auto &entry : m_dynamic_symbols) {
2748 if (entry.symbol.d_tag == tag)
2749 return &entry.symbol;
2750 }
2751 return nullptr;
2752}
2753
2754unsigned ObjectFileELF::PLTRelocationType() {
2755 // DT_PLTREL
2756 // This member specifies the type of relocation entry to which the
2757 // procedure linkage table refers. The d_val member holds DT_REL or
2758 // DT_RELA, as appropriate. All relocations in a procedure linkage table
2759 // must use the same relocation.
2760 const ELFDynamic *symbol = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_PLTREL);
2761
2762 if (symbol)
2763 return symbol->d_val;
2764
2765 return 0;
2766}
2767
2768// Returns the size of the normal plt entries and the offset of the first
2769// normal plt entry. The 0th entry in the plt table is usually a resolution
2770// entry which have different size in some architectures then the rest of the
2771// plt entries.
2772static std::pair<uint64_t, uint64_t>
2773GetPltEntrySizeAndOffset(const ELFSectionHeader *rel_hdr,
2774 const ELFSectionHeader *plt_hdr) {
2775 const elf_xword num_relocations = rel_hdr->sh_size / rel_hdr->sh_entsize;
2776
2777 // Clang 3.3 sets entsize to 4 for 32-bit binaries, but the plt entries are
2778 // 16 bytes. So round the entsize up by the alignment if addralign is set.
2779 elf_xword plt_entsize =
2780 plt_hdr->sh_addralign
2781 ? llvm::alignTo(plt_hdr->sh_entsize, plt_hdr->sh_addralign)
2782 : plt_hdr->sh_entsize;
2783
2784 // Some linkers e.g ld for arm, fill plt_hdr->sh_entsize field incorrectly.
2785 // PLT entries relocation code in general requires multiple instruction and
2786 // should be greater than 4 bytes in most cases. Try to guess correct size
2787 // just in case.
2788 if (plt_entsize <= 4) {
2789 // The linker haven't set the plt_hdr->sh_entsize field. Try to guess the
2790 // size of the plt entries based on the number of entries and the size of
2791 // the plt section with the assumption that the size of the 0th entry is at
2792 // least as big as the size of the normal entries and it isn't much bigger
2793 // then that.
2794 if (plt_hdr->sh_addralign)
2795 plt_entsize = plt_hdr->sh_size / plt_hdr->sh_addralign /
2796 (num_relocations + 1) * plt_hdr->sh_addralign;
2797 else
2798 plt_entsize = plt_hdr->sh_size / (num_relocations + 1);
2799 }
2800
2801 elf_xword plt_offset = plt_hdr->sh_size - num_relocations * plt_entsize;
2802
2803 return std::make_pair(plt_entsize, plt_offset);
2804}
2805
2806static unsigned ParsePLTRelocations(
2807 Symtab *symbol_table, user_id_t start_id, unsigned rel_type,
2808 const ELFHeader *hdr, const ELFSectionHeader *rel_hdr,
2809 const ELFSectionHeader *plt_hdr, const ELFSectionHeader *sym_hdr,
2810 const lldb::SectionSP &plt_section_sp, DataExtractor &rel_data,
2811 DataExtractor &symtab_data, DataExtractor &strtab_data) {
2812 ELFRelocation rel(rel_type);
2813 ELFSymbol symbol;
2814 lldb::offset_t offset = 0;
2815
2816 uint64_t plt_offset, plt_entsize;
2817 std::tie(plt_entsize, plt_offset) =
2818 GetPltEntrySizeAndOffset(rel_hdr, plt_hdr);
2819 const elf_xword num_relocations = rel_hdr->sh_size / rel_hdr->sh_entsize;
2820
2821 typedef unsigned (*reloc_info_fn)(const ELFRelocation &rel);
2822 reloc_info_fn reloc_type;
2823 reloc_info_fn reloc_symbol;
2824
2825 if (hdr->Is32Bit()) {
2826 reloc_type = ELFRelocation::RelocType32;
2827 reloc_symbol = ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol32;
2828 } else {
2829 reloc_type = ELFRelocation::RelocType64;
2830 reloc_symbol = ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol64;
2831 }
2832
2833 unsigned slot_type = hdr->GetRelocationJumpSlotType();
2834 unsigned i;
2835 for (i = 0; i < num_relocations; ++i) {
2836 if (!rel.Parse(rel_data, &offset))
2837 break;
2838
2839 if (reloc_type(rel) != slot_type)
2840 continue;
2841
2842 lldb::offset_t symbol_offset = reloc_symbol(rel) * sym_hdr->sh_entsize;
2843 if (!symbol.Parse(symtab_data, &symbol_offset))
2844 break;
2845
2846 const char *symbol_name = strtab_data.PeekCStr(symbol.st_name);
2847 uint64_t plt_index = plt_offset + i * plt_entsize;
2848
2849 Symbol jump_symbol(
2850 i + start_id, // Symbol table index
2851 symbol_name, // symbol name.
2852 eSymbolTypeTrampoline, // Type of this symbol
2853 false, // Is this globally visible?
2854 false, // Is this symbol debug info?
2855 true, // Is this symbol a trampoline?
2856 true, // Is this symbol artificial?
2857 plt_section_sp, // Section in which this symbol is defined or null.
2858 plt_index, // Offset in section or symbol value.
2859 plt_entsize, // Size in bytes of this symbol.
2860 true, // Size is valid
2861 false, // Contains linker annotations?
2862 0); // Symbol flags.
2863
2864 symbol_table->AddSymbol(jump_symbol);
2865 }
2866
2867 return i;
2868}
2869
2870unsigned
2871ObjectFileELF::ParseTrampolineSymbols(Symtab *symbol_table, user_id_t start_id,
2872 const ELFSectionHeaderInfo *rel_hdr,
2873 user_id_t rel_id) {
2874 assert(rel_hdr->sh_type == SHT_RELA || rel_hdr->sh_type == SHT_REL);
2875
2876 // The link field points to the associated symbol table.
2877 user_id_t symtab_id = rel_hdr->sh_link;
2878
2879 // If the link field doesn't point to the appropriate symbol name table then
2880 // try to find it by name as some compiler don't fill in the link fields.
2881 if (!symtab_id)
2882 symtab_id = GetSectionIndexByName(".dynsym");
2883
2884 // Get PLT section. We cannot use rel_hdr->sh_info, since current linkers
2885 // point that to the .got.plt or .got section instead of .plt.
2886 user_id_t plt_id = GetSectionIndexByName(".plt");
2887
2888 if (!symtab_id || !plt_id)
2889 return 0;
2890
2891 const ELFSectionHeaderInfo *plt_hdr = GetSectionHeaderByIndex(plt_id);
2892 if (!plt_hdr)
2893 return 0;
2894
2895 const ELFSectionHeaderInfo *sym_hdr = GetSectionHeaderByIndex(symtab_id);
2896 if (!sym_hdr)
2897 return 0;
2898
2899 SectionList *section_list = m_sections_up.get();
2900 if (!section_list)
2901 return 0;
2902
2903 Section *rel_section = section_list->FindSectionByID(rel_id).get();
2904 if (!rel_section)
2905 return 0;
2906
2907 SectionSP plt_section_sp(section_list->FindSectionByID(plt_id));
2908 if (!plt_section_sp)
2909 return 0;
2910
2911 Section *symtab = section_list->FindSectionByID(symtab_id).get();
2912 if (!symtab)
2913 return 0;
2914
2915 // sh_link points to associated string table.
2916 Section *strtab = section_list->FindSectionByID(sym_hdr->sh_link).get();
2917 if (!strtab)
2918 return 0;
2919
2920 DataExtractor rel_data;
2921 if (!ReadSectionData(rel_section, rel_data))
2922 return 0;
2923
2924 DataExtractor symtab_data;
2925 if (!ReadSectionData(symtab, symtab_data))
2926 return 0;
2927
2928 DataExtractor strtab_data;
2929 if (!ReadSectionData(strtab, strtab_data))
2930 return 0;
2931
2932 unsigned rel_type = PLTRelocationType();
2933 if (!rel_type)
2934 return 0;
2935
2936 return ParsePLTRelocations(symbol_table, start_id, rel_type, &m_header,
2937 rel_hdr, plt_hdr, sym_hdr, plt_section_sp,
2938 rel_data, symtab_data, strtab_data);
2939}
2940
2941static void ApplyELF64ABS64Relocation(Symtab *symtab, ELFRelocation &rel,
2942 DataExtractor &debug_data,
2943 Section *rel_section) {
2944 const Symbol *symbol =
2945 symtab->FindSymbolByID(ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol64(rel));
2946 if (symbol) {
2947 addr_t value = symbol->GetAddressRef().GetFileAddress();
2948 DataBufferSP data_buffer_sp = debug_data.GetSharedDataBuffer();
2949 // ObjectFileELF creates a WritableDataBuffer in CreateInstance.
2950 WritableDataBuffer *data_buffer =
2951 llvm::cast<WritableDataBuffer>(data_buffer_sp.get());
2952 void *const dst = data_buffer->GetBytes() + rel_section->GetFileOffset() +
2953 ELFRelocation::RelocOffset64(rel);
2954 uint64_t val_offset = value + ELFRelocation::RelocAddend64(rel);
2955 memcpy(dst, &val_offset, sizeof(uint64_t));
2956 }
2957}
2958
2959static void ApplyELF64ABS32Relocation(Symtab *symtab, ELFRelocation &rel,
2960 DataExtractor &debug_data,
2961 Section *rel_section, bool is_signed) {
2962 const Symbol *symbol =
2963 symtab->FindSymbolByID(ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol64(rel));
2964 if (symbol) {
2965 addr_t value = symbol->GetAddressRef().GetFileAddress();
2966 value += ELFRelocation::RelocAddend32(rel);
2967 if ((!is_signed && (value > UINT32_MAX)) ||
2968 (is_signed &&
2969 ((int64_t)value > INT32_MAX || (int64_t)value < INT32_MIN))) {
2970 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Modules);
2971 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Failed to apply debug info relocations");
2972 return;
2973 }
2974 uint32_t truncated_addr = (value & 0xFFFFFFFF);
2975 DataBufferSP data_buffer_sp = debug_data.GetSharedDataBuffer();
2976 // ObjectFileELF creates a WritableDataBuffer in CreateInstance.
2977 WritableDataBuffer *data_buffer =
2978 llvm::cast<WritableDataBuffer>(data_buffer_sp.get());
2979 void *const dst = data_buffer->GetBytes() + rel_section->GetFileOffset() +
2980 ELFRelocation::RelocOffset32(rel);
2981 memcpy(dst, &truncated_addr, sizeof(uint32_t));
2982 }
2983}
2984
2985static void ApplyELF32ABS32RelRelocation(Symtab *symtab, ELFRelocation &rel,
2986 DataExtractor &debug_data,
2987 Section *rel_section) {
2988 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Modules);
2989 const Symbol *symbol =
2990 symtab->FindSymbolByID(ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol32(rel));
2991 if (symbol) {
2992 addr_t value = symbol->GetAddressRef().GetFileAddress();
2993 if (value == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
2994 const char *name = symbol->GetName().GetCString();
2995 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Debug info symbol invalid: %s", name);
2996 return;
2997 }
2998 assert(llvm::isUInt<32>(value) && "Valid addresses are 32-bit");
2999 DataBufferSP data_buffer_sp = debug_data.GetSharedDataBuffer();
3000 // ObjectFileELF creates a WritableDataBuffer in CreateInstance.
3001 WritableDataBuffer *data_buffer =
3002 llvm::cast<WritableDataBuffer>(data_buffer_sp.get());
3003 uint8_t *dst = data_buffer->GetBytes() + rel_section->GetFileOffset() +
3004 ELFRelocation::RelocOffset32(rel);
3005 // Implicit addend is stored inline as a signed value.
3006 int32_t addend;
3007 memcpy(&addend, dst, sizeof(int32_t));
3008 // The sum must be positive. This extra check prevents UB from overflow in
3009 // the actual range check below.
3010 if (addend < 0 && static_cast<uint32_t>(-addend) > value) {
3011 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Debug info relocation overflow: 0x%" PRIx64,
3012 static_cast<int64_t>(value) + addend);
3013 return;
3014 }
3015 if (!llvm::isUInt<32>(value + addend)) {
3016 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Debug info relocation out of range: 0x%" PRIx64, value);
3017 return;
3018 }
3019 uint32_t addr = value + addend;
3020 memcpy(dst, &addr, sizeof(uint32_t));
3021 }
3022}
3023
3024unsigned ObjectFileELF::ApplyRelocations(
3025 Symtab *symtab, const ELFHeader *hdr, const ELFSectionHeader *rel_hdr,
3026 const ELFSectionHeader *symtab_hdr, const ELFSectionHeader *debug_hdr,
3027 DataExtractor &rel_data, DataExtractor &symtab_data,
3028 DataExtractor &debug_data, Section *rel_section) {
3029 ELFRelocation rel(rel_hdr->sh_type);
3030 lldb::addr_t offset = 0;
3031 const unsigned num_relocations = rel_hdr->sh_size / rel_hdr->sh_entsize;
3032 typedef unsigned (*reloc_info_fn)(const ELFRelocation &rel);
3033 reloc_info_fn reloc_type;
3034 reloc_info_fn reloc_symbol;
3035
3036 if (hdr->Is32Bit()) {
3037 reloc_type = ELFRelocation::RelocType32;
3038 reloc_symbol = ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol32;
3039 } else {
3040 reloc_type = ELFRelocation::RelocType64;
3041 reloc_symbol = ELFRelocation::RelocSymbol64;
3042 }
3043
3044 for (unsigned i = 0; i < num_relocations; ++i) {
3045 if (!rel.Parse(rel_data, &offset)) {
3046 GetModule()->ReportError(".rel{0}[{1:d}] failed to parse relocation",
3047 rel_section->GetName().AsCString(), i);
3048 break;
3049 }
3050 const Symbol *symbol = nullptr;
3051
3052 if (hdr->Is32Bit()) {
3053 switch (hdr->e_machine) {
3054 case llvm::ELF::EM_ARM:
3055 switch (reloc_type(rel)) {
3056 case R_ARM_ABS32:
3057 ApplyELF32ABS32RelRelocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section);
3058 break;
3059 case R_ARM_REL32:
3060 GetModule()->ReportError("unsupported AArch32 relocation:"
3061 " .rel{0}[{1}], type {2}",
3062 rel_section->GetName().AsCString(), i,
3063 reloc_type(rel));
3064 break;
3065 default:
3066 assert(false && "unexpected relocation type");
3067 }
3068 break;
3069 case llvm::ELF::EM_386:
3070 switch (reloc_type(rel)) {
3071 case R_386_32:
3072 symbol = symtab->FindSymbolByID(reloc_symbol(rel));
3073 if (symbol) {
3074 addr_t f_offset =
3075 rel_section->GetFileOffset() + ELFRelocation::RelocOffset32(rel);
3076 DataBufferSP data_buffer_sp = debug_data.GetSharedDataBuffer();
3077 // ObjectFileELF creates a WritableDataBuffer in CreateInstance.
3078 WritableDataBuffer *data_buffer =
3079 llvm::cast<WritableDataBuffer>(data_buffer_sp.get());
3080 uint32_t *dst = reinterpret_cast<uint32_t *>(
3081 data_buffer->GetBytes() + f_offset);
3082
3083 addr_t value = symbol->GetAddressRef().GetFileAddress();
3084 if (rel.IsRela()) {
3085 value += ELFRelocation::RelocAddend32(rel);
3086 } else {
3087 value += *dst;
3088 }
3089 *dst = value;
3090 } else {
3091 GetModule()->ReportError(".rel{0}[{1}] unknown symbol id: {2:d}",
3092 rel_section->GetName().AsCString(), i,
3093 reloc_symbol(rel));
3094 }
3095 break;
3096 case R_386_NONE:
3097 case R_386_PC32:
3098 GetModule()->ReportError("unsupported i386 relocation:"
3099 " .rel{0}[{1}], type {2}",
3100 rel_section->GetName().AsCString(), i,
3101 reloc_type(rel));
3102 break;
3103 default:
3104 assert(false && "unexpected relocation type");
3105 break;
3106 }
3107 break;
3108 default:
3109 GetModule()->ReportError("unsupported 32-bit ELF machine arch: {0}", hdr->e_machine);
3110 break;
3111 }
3112 } else {
3113 switch (hdr->e_machine) {
3114 case llvm::ELF::EM_AARCH64:
3115 switch (reloc_type(rel)) {
3116 case R_AARCH64_ABS64:
3117 ApplyELF64ABS64Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section);
3118 break;
3119 case R_AARCH64_ABS32:
3120 ApplyELF64ABS32Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section, true);
3121 break;
3122 default:
3123 assert(false && "unexpected relocation type");
3124 }
3125 break;
3126 case llvm::ELF::EM_LOONGARCH:
3127 switch (reloc_type(rel)) {
3128 case R_LARCH_64:
3129 ApplyELF64ABS64Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section);
3130 break;
3131 case R_LARCH_32:
3132 ApplyELF64ABS32Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section, true);
3133 break;
3134 default:
3135 assert(false && "unexpected relocation type");
3136 }
3137 break;
3138 case llvm::ELF::EM_X86_64:
3139 switch (reloc_type(rel)) {
3140 case R_X86_64_64:
3141 ApplyELF64ABS64Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section);
3142 break;
3143 case R_X86_64_32:
3144 ApplyELF64ABS32Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section,
3145 false);
3146 break;
3147 case R_X86_64_32S:
3148 ApplyELF64ABS32Relocation(symtab, rel, debug_data, rel_section, true);
3149 break;
3150 case R_X86_64_PC32:
3151 default:
3152 assert(false && "unexpected relocation type");
3153 }
3154 break;
3155 default:
3156 GetModule()->ReportError("unsupported 64-bit ELF machine arch: {0}", hdr->e_machine);
3157 break;
3158 }
3159 }
3160 }
3161
3162 return 0;
3163}
3164
3165unsigned ObjectFileELF::RelocateDebugSections(const ELFSectionHeader *rel_hdr,
3166 user_id_t rel_id,
3167 lldb_private::Symtab *thetab) {
3168 assert(rel_hdr->sh_type == SHT_RELA || rel_hdr->sh_type == SHT_REL);
3169
3170 // Parse in the section list if needed.
3171 SectionList *section_list = GetSectionList();
3172 if (!section_list)
3173 return 0;
3174
3175 user_id_t symtab_id = rel_hdr->sh_link;
3176 user_id_t debug_id = rel_hdr->sh_info;
3177
3178 const ELFSectionHeader *symtab_hdr = GetSectionHeaderByIndex(symtab_id);
3179 if (!symtab_hdr)
3180 return 0;
3181
3182 const ELFSectionHeader *debug_hdr = GetSectionHeaderByIndex(debug_id);
3183 if (!debug_hdr)
3184 return 0;
3185
3186 Section *rel = section_list->FindSectionByID(rel_id).get();
3187 if (!rel)
3188 return 0;
3189
3190 Section *symtab = section_list->FindSectionByID(symtab_id).get();
3191 if (!symtab)
3192 return 0;
3193
3194 Section *debug = section_list->FindSectionByID(debug_id).get();
3195 if (!debug)
3196 return 0;
3197
3198 DataExtractorSP rel_data_sp = std::make_shared<DataExtractor>();
3199 DataExtractorSP symtab_data_sp = std::make_shared<DataExtractor>();
3200 DataExtractorSP debug_data_sp = std::make_shared<DataExtractor>();
3201
3202 if (GetData(rel->GetFileOffset(), rel->GetFileSize(), rel_data_sp) &&
3203 GetData(symtab->GetFileOffset(), symtab->GetFileSize(), symtab_data_sp) &&
3204 GetData(debug->GetFileOffset(), debug->GetFileSize(), debug_data_sp)) {
3205 ApplyRelocations(thetab, &m_header, rel_hdr, symtab_hdr, debug_hdr,
3206 *rel_data_sp, *symtab_data_sp, *debug_data_sp, debug);
3207 }
3208
3209 return 0;
3210}
3211
3212void ObjectFileELF::ParseSymtab(Symtab &lldb_symtab) {
3213 ModuleSP module_sp(GetModule());
3214 if (!module_sp)
3215 return;
3216
3217 Progress progress("Parsing symbol table",
3218 m_file.GetFilename().AsCString("<Unknown>"));
3219 ElapsedTime elapsed(module_sp->GetSymtabParseTime());
3220
3221 // We always want to use the main object file so we (hopefully) only have one
3222 // cached copy of our symtab, dynamic sections, etc.
3223 ObjectFile *module_obj_file = module_sp->GetObjectFile();
3224 if (module_obj_file && module_obj_file != this)
3225 return module_obj_file->ParseSymtab(lldb_symtab);
3226
3227 SectionList *section_list = module_sp->GetSectionList();
3228 if (!section_list)
3229 return;
3230
3231 uint64_t symbol_id = 0;
3232
3233 // Sharable objects and dynamic executables usually have 2 distinct symbol
3234 // tables, one named ".symtab", and the other ".dynsym". The dynsym is a
3235 // smaller version of the symtab that only contains global symbols. The
3236 // information found in the dynsym is therefore also found in the symtab,
3237 // while the reverse is not necessarily true.
3238 Section *symtab =
3239 section_list->FindSectionByType(eSectionTypeELFSymbolTable, true).get();
3240 if (symtab) {
3241 auto [num_symbols, address_class_map] =
3242 ParseSymbolTable(&lldb_symtab, symbol_id, symtab);
3243 m_address_class_map.merge(address_class_map);
3244 symbol_id += num_symbols;
3245 }
3246
3247 // The symtab section is non-allocable and can be stripped, while the
3248 // .dynsym section which should always be always be there. To support the
3249 // minidebuginfo case we parse .dynsym when there's a .gnu_debuginfo
3250 // section, nomatter if .symtab was already parsed or not. This is because
3251 // minidebuginfo normally removes the .symtab symbols which have their
3252 // matching .dynsym counterparts.
3253 if (!symtab ||
3254 GetSectionList()->FindSectionByName(ConstString(".gnu_debugdata"))) {
3255 Section *dynsym =
3256 section_list->FindSectionByType(eSectionTypeELFDynamicSymbols, true)
3257 .get();
3258 if (dynsym) {
3259 auto [num_symbols, address_class_map] =
3260 ParseSymbolTable(&lldb_symtab, symbol_id, dynsym);
3261 symbol_id += num_symbols;
3262 m_address_class_map.merge(address_class_map);
3263 } else {
3264 // Try and read the dynamic symbol table from the .dynamic section.
3265 uint32_t dynamic_num_symbols = 0;
3266 std::optional<DataExtractor> symtab_data =
3267 GetDynsymDataFromDynamic(dynamic_num_symbols);
3268 std::optional<DataExtractor> strtab_data = GetDynstrData();
3269 if (symtab_data && strtab_data) {
3270 auto [num_symbols_parsed, address_class_map] = ParseSymbols(
3271 &lldb_symtab, symbol_id, section_list, dynamic_num_symbols,
3272 symtab_data.value(), strtab_data.value());
3273 symbol_id += num_symbols_parsed;
3274 m_address_class_map.merge(address_class_map);
3275 }
3276 }
3277 }
3278
3279 // DT_JMPREL
3280 // If present, this entry's d_ptr member holds the address of
3281 // relocation
3282 // entries associated solely with the procedure linkage table.
3283 // Separating
3284 // these relocation entries lets the dynamic linker ignore them during
3285 // process initialization, if lazy binding is enabled. If this entry is
3286 // present, the related entries of types DT_PLTRELSZ and DT_PLTREL must
3287 // also be present.
3288 const ELFDynamic *symbol = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_JMPREL);
3289 if (symbol) {
3290 // Synthesize trampoline symbols to help navigate the PLT.
3291 addr_t addr = symbol->d_ptr;
3292 Section *reloc_section =
3293 section_list->FindSectionContainingFileAddress(addr).get();
3294 if (reloc_section) {
3295 user_id_t reloc_id = reloc_section->GetID();
3296 const ELFSectionHeaderInfo *reloc_header =
3297 GetSectionHeaderByIndex(reloc_id);
3298 if (reloc_header)
3299 ParseTrampolineSymbols(&lldb_symtab, symbol_id, reloc_header, reloc_id);
3300 }
3301 }
3302
3303 if (DWARFCallFrameInfo *eh_frame =
3304 GetModule()->GetUnwindTable().GetEHFrameInfo()) {
3305 ParseUnwindSymbols(&lldb_symtab, eh_frame);
3306 }
3307
3308 // In the event that there's no symbol entry for the entry point we'll
3309 // artificially create one. We delegate to the symtab object the figuring
3310 // out of the proper size, this will usually make it span til the next
3311 // symbol it finds in the section. This means that if there are missing
3312 // symbols the entry point might span beyond its function definition.
3313 // We're fine with this as it doesn't make it worse than not having a
3314 // symbol entry at all.
3315 if (CalculateType() == eTypeExecutable) {
3316 ArchSpec arch = GetArchitecture();
3317 auto entry_point_addr = GetEntryPointAddress();
3318 bool is_valid_entry_point =
3319 entry_point_addr.IsValid() && entry_point_addr.IsSectionOffset();
3320 addr_t entry_point_file_addr = entry_point_addr.GetFileAddress();
3321 if (is_valid_entry_point && !lldb_symtab.FindSymbolContainingFileAddress(
3322 entry_point_file_addr)) {
3323 uint64_t symbol_id = lldb_symtab.GetNumSymbols();
3324 // Don't set the name for any synthetic symbols, the Symbol
3325 // object will generate one if needed when the name is accessed
3326 // via accessors.
3327 SectionSP section_sp = entry_point_addr.GetSection();
3328 Symbol symbol(
3329 /*symID=*/symbol_id,
3330 /*name=*/llvm::StringRef(), // Name will be auto generated.
3331 /*type=*/eSymbolTypeCode,
3332 /*external=*/true,
3333 /*is_debug=*/false,
3334 /*is_trampoline=*/false,
3335 /*is_artificial=*/true,
3336 /*section_sp=*/section_sp,
3337 /*offset=*/0,
3338 /*size=*/0, // FDE can span multiple symbols so don't use its size.
3339 /*size_is_valid=*/false,
3340 /*contains_linker_annotations=*/false,
3341 /*flags=*/0);
3342 // When the entry point is arm thumb we need to explicitly set its
3343 // class address to reflect that. This is important because expression
3344 // evaluation relies on correctly setting a breakpoint at this
3345 // address.
3346 if (arch.GetMachine() == llvm::Triple::arm &&
3347 (entry_point_file_addr & 1)) {
3348 symbol.GetAddressRef().SetOffset(entry_point_addr.GetOffset() ^ 1);
3349 m_address_class_map[entry_point_file_addr ^ 1] =
3350 AddressClass::eCodeAlternateISA;
3351 } else {
3352 m_address_class_map[entry_point_file_addr] = AddressClass::eCode;
3353 }
3354 lldb_symtab.AddSymbol(symbol);
3355 }
3356 }
3357}
3358
3359void ObjectFileELF::RelocateSection(lldb_private::Section *section)
3360{
3361 static const char *debug_prefix = ".debug";
3362
3363 // Set relocated bit so we stop getting called, regardless of whether we
3364 // actually relocate.
3365 section->SetIsRelocated(true);
3366
3367 // We only relocate in ELF relocatable files
3368 if (CalculateType() != eTypeObjectFile)
3369 return;
3370
3371 const char *section_name = section->GetName().GetCString();
3372 // Can't relocate that which can't be named
3373 if (section_name == nullptr)
3374 return;
3375
3376 // We don't relocate non-debug sections at the moment
3377 if (strncmp(section_name, debug_prefix, strlen(debug_prefix)))
3378 return;
3379
3380 // Relocation section names to look for
3381 std::string needle = std::string(".rel") + section_name;
3382 std::string needlea = std::string(".rela") + section_name;
3383
3384 for (SectionHeaderCollIter I = m_section_headers.begin();
3385 I != m_section_headers.end(); ++I) {
3386 if (I->sh_type == SHT_RELA || I->sh_type == SHT_REL) {
3387 const char *hay_name = I->section_name.GetCString();
3388 if (hay_name == nullptr)
3389 continue;
3390 if (needle == hay_name || needlea == hay_name) {
3391 const ELFSectionHeader &reloc_header = *I;
3392 user_id_t reloc_id = SectionIndex(I);
3393 RelocateDebugSections(&reloc_header, reloc_id, GetSymtab());
3394 break;
3395 }
3396 }
3397 }
3398}
3399
3400void ObjectFileELF::ParseUnwindSymbols(Symtab *symbol_table,
3401 DWARFCallFrameInfo *eh_frame) {
3402 SectionList *section_list = GetSectionList();
3403 if (!section_list)
3404 return;
3405
3406 // First we save the new symbols into a separate list and add them to the
3407 // symbol table after we collected all symbols we want to add. This is
3408 // neccessary because adding a new symbol invalidates the internal index of
3409 // the symtab what causing the next lookup to be slow because it have to
3410 // recalculate the index first.
3411 std::vector<Symbol> new_symbols;
3412
3413 size_t num_symbols = symbol_table->GetNumSymbols();
3414 uint64_t last_symbol_id =
3415 num_symbols ? symbol_table->SymbolAtIndex(num_symbols - 1)->GetID() : 0;
3416 eh_frame->ForEachFDEEntries([&](lldb::addr_t file_addr, uint32_t size,
3417 dw_offset_t) {
3418 Symbol *symbol = symbol_table->FindSymbolAtFileAddress(file_addr);
3419 if (symbol) {
3420 if (!symbol->GetByteSizeIsValid()) {
3421 symbol->SetByteSize(size);
3422 symbol->SetSizeIsSynthesized(true);
3423 }
3424 } else {
3425 SectionSP section_sp =
3426 section_list->FindSectionContainingFileAddress(file_addr);
3427 if (section_sp) {
3428 addr_t offset = file_addr - section_sp->GetFileAddress();
3429 uint64_t symbol_id = ++last_symbol_id;
3430 // Don't set the name for any synthetic symbols, the Symbol
3431 // object will generate one if needed when the name is accessed
3432 // via accessors.
3433 Symbol eh_symbol(
3434 /*symID=*/symbol_id,
3435 /*name=*/llvm::StringRef(), // Name will be auto generated.
3436 /*type=*/eSymbolTypeCode,
3437 /*external=*/true,
3438 /*is_debug=*/false,
3439 /*is_trampoline=*/false,
3440 /*is_artificial=*/true,
3441 /*section_sp=*/section_sp,
3442 /*offset=*/offset,
3443 /*size=*/0, // FDE can span multiple symbols so don't use its size.
3444 /*size_is_valid=*/false,
3445 /*contains_linker_annotations=*/false,
3446 /*flags=*/0);
3447 new_symbols.push_back(eh_symbol);
3448 }
3449 }
3450 return true;
3451 });
3452
3453 for (const Symbol &s : new_symbols)
3454 symbol_table->AddSymbol(s);
3455}
3456
3457bool ObjectFileELF::IsStripped() {
3458 // TODO: determine this for ELF
3459 return false;
3460}
3461
3462//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
3463// Dump
3464//
3465// Dump the specifics of the runtime file container (such as any headers
3466// segments, sections, etc).
3467void ObjectFileELF::Dump(Stream *s) {
3468 ModuleSP module_sp(GetModule());
3469 if (!module_sp) {
3470 return;
3471 }
3472
3473 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(module_sp->GetMutex());
3474 s->Printf("%p: ", static_cast<void *>(this));
3475 s->Indent();
3476 s->PutCString("ObjectFileELF");
3477
3478 ArchSpec header_arch = GetArchitecture();
3479
3480 *s << ", file = '" << m_file
3481 << "', arch = " << header_arch.GetArchitectureName();
3482 if (m_memory_addr != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
3483 s->Printf(", addr = %#16.16" PRIx64, m_memory_addr);
3484 s->EOL();
3485
3486 DumpELFHeader(s, m_header);
3487 s->EOL();
3488 DumpELFProgramHeaders(s);
3489 s->EOL();
3490 DumpELFSectionHeaders(s);
3491 s->EOL();
3492 SectionList *section_list = GetSectionList();
3493 if (section_list)
3494 section_list->Dump(s->AsRawOstream(), s->GetIndentLevel(), nullptr, true,
3495 UINT32_MAX);
3496 Symtab *symtab = GetSymtab();
3497 if (symtab)
3498 symtab->Dump(s, nullptr, eSortOrderNone);
3499 s->EOL();
3500 DumpDependentModules(s);
3501 s->EOL();
3502 DumpELFDynamic(s);
3503 s->EOL();
3504 Address image_info_addr = GetImageInfoAddress(nullptr);
3505 if (image_info_addr.IsValid())
3506 s->Printf("image_info_address = %#16.16" PRIx64 "\n",
3507 image_info_addr.GetFileAddress());
3508}
3509
3510// DumpELFHeader
3511//
3512// Dump the ELF header to the specified output stream
3513void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFHeader(Stream *s, const ELFHeader &header) {
3514 s->PutCString("ELF Header\n");
3515 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_MAG0 ] = 0x%2.2x\n", header.e_ident[EI_MAG0]);
3516 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_MAG1 ] = 0x%2.2x '%c'\n", header.e_ident[EI_MAG1],
3517 header.e_ident[EI_MAG1]);
3518 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_MAG2 ] = 0x%2.2x '%c'\n", header.e_ident[EI_MAG2],
3519 header.e_ident[EI_MAG2]);
3520 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_MAG3 ] = 0x%2.2x '%c'\n", header.e_ident[EI_MAG3],
3521 header.e_ident[EI_MAG3]);
3522
3523 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_CLASS ] = 0x%2.2x\n", header.e_ident[EI_CLASS]);
3524 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_DATA ] = 0x%2.2x ", header.e_ident[EI_DATA]);
3525 DumpELFHeader_e_ident_EI_DATA(s, header.e_ident[EI_DATA]);
3526 s->Printf("\ne_ident[EI_VERSION] = 0x%2.2x\n", header.e_ident[EI_VERSION]);
3527 s->Printf("e_ident[EI_PAD ] = 0x%2.2x\n", header.e_ident[EI_PAD]);
3528
3529 s->Printf("e_type = 0x%4.4x ", header.e_type);
3530 DumpELFHeader_e_type(s, header.e_type);
3531 s->Printf("\ne_machine = 0x%4.4x\n", header.e_machine);
3532 s->Printf("e_version = 0x%8.8x\n", header.e_version);
3533 s->Printf("e_entry = 0x%8.8" PRIx64 "\n", header.e_entry);
3534 s->Printf("e_phoff = 0x%8.8" PRIx64 "\n", header.e_phoff);
3535 s->Printf("e_shoff = 0x%8.8" PRIx64 "\n", header.e_shoff);
3536 s->Printf("e_flags = 0x%8.8x\n", header.e_flags);
3537 s->Printf("e_ehsize = 0x%4.4x\n", header.e_ehsize);
3538 s->Printf("e_phentsize = 0x%4.4x\n", header.e_phentsize);
3539 s->Printf("e_phnum = 0x%8.8x\n", header.e_phnum);
3540 s->Printf("e_shentsize = 0x%4.4x\n", header.e_shentsize);
3541 s->Printf("e_shnum = 0x%8.8x\n", header.e_shnum);
3542 s->Printf("e_shstrndx = 0x%8.8x\n", header.e_shstrndx);
3543}
3544
3545// DumpELFHeader_e_type
3546//
3547// Dump an token value for the ELF header member e_type
3548void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFHeader_e_type(Stream *s, elf_half e_type) {
3549 switch (e_type) {
3550 case ET_NONE:
3551 *s << "ET_NONE";
3552 break;
3553 case ET_REL:
3554 *s << "ET_REL";
3555 break;
3556 case ET_EXEC:
3557 *s << "ET_EXEC";
3558 break;
3559 case ET_DYN:
3560 *s << "ET_DYN";
3561 break;
3562 case ET_CORE:
3563 *s << "ET_CORE";
3564 break;
3565 default:
3566 break;
3567 }
3568}
3569
3570// DumpELFHeader_e_ident_EI_DATA
3571//
3572// Dump an token value for the ELF header member e_ident[EI_DATA]
3573void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFHeader_e_ident_EI_DATA(Stream *s,
3574 unsigned char ei_data) {
3575 switch (ei_data) {
3576 case ELFDATANONE:
3577 *s << "ELFDATANONE";
3578 break;
3579 case ELFDATA2LSB:
3580 *s << "ELFDATA2LSB - Little Endian";
3581 break;
3582 case ELFDATA2MSB:
3583 *s << "ELFDATA2MSB - Big Endian";
3584 break;
3585 default:
3586 break;
3587 }
3588}
3589
3590// DumpELFProgramHeader
3591//
3592// Dump a single ELF program header to the specified output stream
3593void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFProgramHeader(Stream *s,
3594 const ELFProgramHeader &ph) {
3595 DumpELFProgramHeader_p_type(s, ph.p_type);
3596 s->Printf(" %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8" PRIx64, ph.p_offset,
3597 ph.p_vaddr, ph.p_paddr);
3598 s->Printf(" %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8x (", ph.p_filesz, ph.p_memsz,
3599 ph.p_flags);
3600
3601 DumpELFProgramHeader_p_flags(s, ph.p_flags);
3602 s->Printf(") %8.8" PRIx64, ph.p_align);
3603}
3604
3605// DumpELFProgramHeader_p_type
3606//
3607// Dump an token value for the ELF program header member p_type which describes
3608// the type of the program header
3609void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFProgramHeader_p_type(Stream *s, elf_word p_type) {
3610 const int kStrWidth = 15;
3611 switch (p_type) {
3612 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_NULL, kStrWidth);
3613 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_LOAD, kStrWidth);
3614 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_DYNAMIC, kStrWidth);
3615 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_INTERP, kStrWidth);
3616 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_NOTE, kStrWidth);
3617 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_SHLIB, kStrWidth);
3618 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_PHDR, kStrWidth);
3619 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_TLS, kStrWidth);
3620 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, PT_GNU_EH_FRAME, kStrWidth);
3621 default:
3622 s->Printf("0x%8.8x%*s", p_type, kStrWidth - 10, "");
3623 break;
3624 }
3625}
3626
3627// DumpELFProgramHeader_p_flags
3628//
3629// Dump an token value for the ELF program header member p_flags
3630void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFProgramHeader_p_flags(Stream *s, elf_word p_flags) {
3631 *s << ((p_flags & PF_X) ? "PF_X" : " ")
3632 << (((p_flags & PF_X) && (p_flags & PF_W)) ? '+' : ' ')
3633 << ((p_flags & PF_W) ? "PF_W" : " ")
3634 << (((p_flags & PF_W) && (p_flags & PF_R)) ? '+' : ' ')
3635 << ((p_flags & PF_R) ? "PF_R" : " ");
3636}
3637
3638// DumpELFProgramHeaders
3639//
3640// Dump all of the ELF program header to the specified output stream
3641void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFProgramHeaders(Stream *s) {
3642 if (!ParseProgramHeaders())
3643 return;
3644
3645 s->PutCString("Program Headers\n");
3646 s->PutCString("IDX p_type p_offset p_vaddr p_paddr "
3647 "p_filesz p_memsz p_flags p_align\n");
3648 s->PutCString("==== --------------- -------- -------- -------- "
3649 "-------- -------- ------------------------- --------\n");
3650
3651 for (const auto &H : llvm::enumerate(m_program_headers)) {
3652 s->Format("[{0,2}] ", H.index());
3653 ObjectFileELF::DumpELFProgramHeader(s, H.value());
3654 s->EOL();
3655 }
3656}
3657
3658// DumpELFSectionHeader
3659//
3660// Dump a single ELF section header to the specified output stream
3661void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFSectionHeader(Stream *s,
3662 const ELFSectionHeaderInfo &sh) {
3663 s->Printf("%8.8x ", sh.sh_name);
3664 DumpELFSectionHeader_sh_type(s, sh.sh_type);
3665 s->Printf(" %8.8" PRIx64 " (", sh.sh_flags);
3666 DumpELFSectionHeader_sh_flags(s, sh.sh_flags);
3667 s->Printf(") %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8" PRIx64, sh.sh_addr,
3668 sh.sh_offset, sh.sh_size);
3669 s->Printf(" %8.8x %8.8x", sh.sh_link, sh.sh_info);
3670 s->Printf(" %8.8" PRIx64 " %8.8" PRIx64, sh.sh_addralign, sh.sh_entsize);
3671}
3672
3673// DumpELFSectionHeader_sh_type
3674//
3675// Dump an token value for the ELF section header member sh_type which
3676// describes the type of the section
3677void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFSectionHeader_sh_type(Stream *s, elf_word sh_type) {
3678 const int kStrWidth = 12;
3679 switch (sh_type) {
3680 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_NULL, kStrWidth);
3681 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_PROGBITS, kStrWidth);
3682 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_SYMTAB, kStrWidth);
3683 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_STRTAB, kStrWidth);
3684 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_RELA, kStrWidth);
3685 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_HASH, kStrWidth);
3686 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_DYNAMIC, kStrWidth);
3687 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_NOTE, kStrWidth);
3688 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_NOBITS, kStrWidth);
3689 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_REL, kStrWidth);
3690 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_SHLIB, kStrWidth);
3691 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_DYNSYM, kStrWidth);
3692 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_LOPROC, kStrWidth);
3693 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_HIPROC, kStrWidth);
3694 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_LOUSER, kStrWidth);
3695 CASE_AND_STREAM(s, SHT_HIUSER, kStrWidth);
3696 default:
3697 s->Printf("0x%8.8x%*s", sh_type, kStrWidth - 10, "");
3698 break;
3699 }
3700}
3701
3702// DumpELFSectionHeader_sh_flags
3703//
3704// Dump an token value for the ELF section header member sh_flags
3705void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFSectionHeader_sh_flags(Stream *s,
3706 elf_xword sh_flags) {
3707 *s << ((sh_flags & SHF_WRITE) ? "WRITE" : " ")
3708 << (((sh_flags & SHF_WRITE) && (sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC)) ? '+' : ' ')
3709 << ((sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) ? "ALLOC" : " ")
3710 << (((sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) && (sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR)) ? '+' : ' ')
3711 << ((sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) ? "EXECINSTR" : " ");
3712}
3713
3714// DumpELFSectionHeaders
3715//
3716// Dump all of the ELF section header to the specified output stream
3717void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFSectionHeaders(Stream *s) {
3718 if (!ParseSectionHeaders())
3719 return;
3720
3721 s->PutCString("Section Headers\n");
3722 s->PutCString("IDX name type flags "
3723 "addr offset size link info addralgn "
3724 "entsize Name\n");
3725 s->PutCString("==== -------- ------------ -------------------------------- "
3726 "-------- -------- -------- -------- -------- -------- "
3727 "-------- ====================\n");
3728
3729 uint32_t idx = 0;
3730 for (SectionHeaderCollConstIter I = m_section_headers.begin();
3731 I != m_section_headers.end(); ++I, ++idx) {
3732 s->Printf("[%2u] ", idx);
3733 ObjectFileELF::DumpELFSectionHeader(s, *I);
3734 const char *section_name = I->section_name.AsCString("");
3735 if (section_name)
3736 *s << ' ' << section_name << "\n";
3737 }
3738}
3739
3740void ObjectFileELF::DumpDependentModules(lldb_private::Stream *s) {
3741 size_t num_modules = ParseDependentModules();
3742
3743 if (num_modules > 0) {
3744 s->PutCString("Dependent Modules:\n");
3745 for (unsigned i = 0; i < num_modules; ++i) {
3746 const FileSpec &spec = m_filespec_up->GetFileSpecAtIndex(i);
3747 s->Printf(" %s\n", spec.GetFilename().GetCString());
3748 }
3749 }
3750}
3751
3752std::string static getDynamicTagAsString(uint16_t Arch, uint64_t Type) {
3753#define DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(tag, value) \
3754 case value: \
3755 return #tag;
3756
3757#define DYNAMIC_TAG(n, v)
3758 switch (Arch) {
3759 case llvm::ELF::EM_AARCH64:
3760 switch (Type) {
3761#define AARCH64_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(name, value)
3762#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3763#undef AARCH64_DYNAMIC_TAG
3764 }
3765 break;
3766
3767 case llvm::ELF::EM_HEXAGON:
3768 switch (Type) {
3769#define HEXAGON_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(name, value)
3770#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3771#undef HEXAGON_DYNAMIC_TAG
3772 }
3773 break;
3774
3775 case llvm::ELF::EM_MIPS:
3776 switch (Type) {
3777#define MIPS_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(name, value)
3778#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3779#undef MIPS_DYNAMIC_TAG
3780 }
3781 break;
3782
3783 case llvm::ELF::EM_PPC:
3784 switch (Type) {
3785#define PPC_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(name, value)
3786#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3787#undef PPC_DYNAMIC_TAG
3788 }
3789 break;
3790
3791 case llvm::ELF::EM_PPC64:
3792 switch (Type) {
3793#define PPC64_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(name, value)
3794#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3795#undef PPC64_DYNAMIC_TAG
3796 }
3797 break;
3798
3799 case llvm::ELF::EM_RISCV:
3800 switch (Type) {
3801#define RISCV_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM(name, value)
3802#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3803#undef RISCV_DYNAMIC_TAG
3804 }
3805 break;
3806 }
3807#undef DYNAMIC_TAG
3808 switch (Type) {
3809// Now handle all dynamic tags except the architecture specific ones
3810#define AARCH64_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value)
3811#define MIPS_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value)
3812#define HEXAGON_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value)
3813#define PPC_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value)
3814#define PPC64_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value)
3815#define RISCV_DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value)
3816// Also ignore marker tags such as DT_HIOS (maps to DT_VERNEEDNUM), etc.
3817#define DYNAMIC_TAG_MARKER(name, value)
3818#define DYNAMIC_TAG(name, value) \
3819 case value: \
3820 return #name;
3821#include "llvm/BinaryFormat/DynamicTags.def"
3822#undef DYNAMIC_TAG
3823#undef AARCH64_DYNAMIC_TAG
3824#undef MIPS_DYNAMIC_TAG
3825#undef HEXAGON_DYNAMIC_TAG
3826#undef PPC_DYNAMIC_TAG
3827#undef PPC64_DYNAMIC_TAG
3828#undef RISCV_DYNAMIC_TAG
3829#undef DYNAMIC_TAG_MARKER
3830#undef DYNAMIC_STRINGIFY_ENUM
3831 default:
3832 return "<unknown:>0x" + llvm::utohexstr(Type, true);
3833 }
3834}
3835
3836void ObjectFileELF::DumpELFDynamic(lldb_private::Stream *s) {
3837 ParseDynamicSymbols();
3838 if (m_dynamic_symbols.empty())
3839 return;
3840
3841 s->PutCString(".dynamic:\n");
3842 s->PutCString("IDX d_tag d_val/d_ptr\n");
3843 s->PutCString("==== ---------------- ------------------\n");
3844 uint32_t idx = 0;
3845 for (const auto &entry : m_dynamic_symbols) {
3846 s->Printf("[%2u] ", idx++);
3847 s->Printf(
3848 "%-16s 0x%16.16" PRIx64,
3849 getDynamicTagAsString(m_header.e_machine, entry.symbol.d_tag).c_str(),
3850 entry.symbol.d_ptr);
3851 if (!entry.name.empty())
3852 s->Printf(" \"%s\"", entry.name.c_str());
3853 s->EOL();
3854 }
3855}
3856
3857ArchSpec ObjectFileELF::GetArchitecture() {
3858 if (!ParseHeader())
3859 return ArchSpec();
3860
3861 if (m_section_headers.empty()) {
3862 // Allow elf notes to be parsed which may affect the detected architecture.
3863 ParseSectionHeaders();
3864 }
3865
3866 if (CalculateType() == eTypeCoreFile &&
3867 !m_arch_spec.TripleOSWasSpecified()) {
3868 // Core files don't have section headers yet they have PT_NOTE program
3869 // headers that might shed more light on the architecture
3870 for (const elf::ELFProgramHeader &H : ProgramHeaders()) {
3871 if (H.p_type != PT_NOTE || H.p_offset == 0 || H.p_filesz == 0)
3872 continue;
3873 DataExtractor data;
3874 if (data.SetData(*m_data_nsp, H.p_offset, H.p_filesz) == H.p_filesz) {
3875 UUID uuid;
3876 RefineModuleDetailsFromNote(data, m_arch_spec, uuid);
3877 }
3878 }
3879 }
3880 return m_arch_spec;
3881}
3882
3883ObjectFile::Type ObjectFileELF::CalculateType() {
3884 switch (m_header.e_type) {
3885 case llvm::ELF::ET_NONE:
3886 // 0 - No file type
3887 return eTypeUnknown;
3888
3889 case llvm::ELF::ET_REL:
3890 // 1 - Relocatable file
3891 return eTypeObjectFile;
3892
3893 case llvm::ELF::ET_EXEC:
3894 // 2 - Executable file
3895 return eTypeExecutable;
3896
3897 case llvm::ELF::ET_DYN:
3898 // 3 - Shared object file
3899 return eTypeSharedLibrary;
3900
3901 case ET_CORE:
3902 // 4 - Core file
3903 return eTypeCoreFile;
3904
3905 default:
3906 break;
3907 }
3908 return eTypeUnknown;
3909}
3910
3911ObjectFile::Strata ObjectFileELF::CalculateStrata() {
3912 switch (m_header.e_type) {
3913 case llvm::ELF::ET_NONE:
3914 // 0 - No file type
3915 return eStrataUnknown;
3916
3917 case llvm::ELF::ET_REL:
3918 // 1 - Relocatable file
3919 return eStrataUnknown;
3920
3921 case llvm::ELF::ET_EXEC:
3922 // 2 - Executable file
3923 {
3924 SectionList *section_list = GetSectionList();
3925 if (section_list) {
3926 static ConstString loader_section_name(".interp");
3927 SectionSP loader_section =
3928 section_list->FindSectionByName(loader_section_name);
3929 if (loader_section) {
3930 char buffer[256];
3931 size_t read_size =
3932 ReadSectionData(loader_section.get(), 0, buffer, sizeof(buffer));
3933
3934 // We compare the content of .interp section
3935 // It will contains \0 when counting read_size, so the size needs to
3936 // decrease by one
3937 llvm::StringRef loader_name(buffer, read_size - 1);
3938 llvm::StringRef freebsd_kernel_loader_name("/red/herring");
3939 if (loader_name == freebsd_kernel_loader_name)
3940 return eStrataKernel;
3941 }
3942 }
3943 return eStrataUser;
3944 }
3945
3946 case llvm::ELF::ET_DYN:
3947 // 3 - Shared object file
3948 // TODO: is there any way to detect that an shared library is a kernel
3949 // related executable by inspecting the program headers, section headers,
3950 // symbols, or any other flag bits???
3951 return eStrataUnknown;
3952
3953 case ET_CORE:
3954 // 4 - Core file
3955 // TODO: is there any way to detect that an core file is a kernel
3956 // related executable by inspecting the program headers, section headers,
3957 // symbols, or any other flag bits???
3958 return eStrataUnknown;
3959
3960 default:
3961 break;
3962 }
3963 return eStrataUnknown;
3964}
3965
3966size_t ObjectFileELF::ReadSectionData(Section *section,
3967 lldb::offset_t section_offset, void *dst,
3968 size_t dst_len) {
3969 // If some other objectfile owns this data, pass this to them.
3970 if (section->GetObjectFile() != this)
3971 return section->GetObjectFile()->ReadSectionData(section, section_offset,
3972 dst, dst_len);
3973
3974 if (!section->Test(SHF_COMPRESSED))
3975 return ObjectFile::ReadSectionData(section, section_offset, dst, dst_len);
3976
3977 // For compressed sections we need to read to full data to be able to
3978 // decompress.
3979 DataExtractor data;
3980 ReadSectionData(section, data);
3981 return data.CopyData(section_offset, dst_len, dst);
3982}
3983
3984size_t ObjectFileELF::ReadSectionData(Section *section,
3985 DataExtractor &section_data) {
3986 // If some other objectfile owns this data, pass this to them.
3987 if (section->GetObjectFile() != this)
3988 return section->GetObjectFile()->ReadSectionData(section, section_data);
3989
3990 size_t result = ObjectFile::ReadSectionData(section, section_data);
3991 if (result == 0 || !(section->Get() & llvm::ELF::SHF_COMPRESSED))
3992 return result;
3993
3994 auto Decompressor = llvm::object::Decompressor::create(
3995 section->GetName().GetStringRef(),
3996 {reinterpret_cast<const char *>(section_data.GetDataStart()),
3997 size_t(section_data.GetByteSize())},
3998 GetByteOrder() == eByteOrderLittle, GetAddressByteSize() == 8);
3999 if (!Decompressor) {
4000 GetModule()->ReportWarning(
4001 "Unable to initialize decompressor for section '{0}': {1}",
4002 section->GetName().GetCString(),
4003 llvm::toString(Decompressor.takeError()).c_str());
4004 section_data.Clear();
4005 return 0;
4006 }
4007
4008 auto buffer_sp =
4009 std::make_shared<DataBufferHeap>(Decompressor->getDecompressedSize(), 0);
4010 if (auto error = Decompressor->decompress(
4011 {buffer_sp->GetBytes(), size_t(buffer_sp->GetByteSize())})) {
4012 GetModule()->ReportWarning("Decompression of section '{0}' failed: {1}",
4013 section->GetName().GetCString(),
4014 llvm::toString(std::move(error)).c_str());
4015 section_data.Clear();
4016 return 0;
4017 }
4018
4019 section_data.SetData(buffer_sp);
4020 return buffer_sp->GetByteSize();
4021}
4022
4023llvm::ArrayRef<ELFProgramHeader> ObjectFileELF::ProgramHeaders() {
4024 ParseProgramHeaders();
4025 return m_program_headers;
4026}
4027
4028DataExtractor ObjectFileELF::GetSegmentData(const ELFProgramHeader &H) {
4029 // Try and read the program header from our cached m_data_nsp which can come
4030 // from the file on disk being mmap'ed or from the initial part of the ELF
4031 // file we read from memory and cached.
4032 DataExtractor data = DataExtractor(*m_data_nsp, H.p_offset, H.p_filesz);
4033 if (data.GetByteSize() == H.p_filesz)
4034 return data;
4035 if (IsInMemory()) {
4036 // We have a ELF file in process memory, read the program header data from
4037 // the process.
4038 if (ProcessSP process_sp = m_process_wp.lock()) {
4039 const lldb::offset_t base_file_addr = GetBaseAddress().GetFileAddress();
4040 const addr_t load_bias = m_memory_addr - base_file_addr;
4041 const addr_t data_addr = H.p_vaddr + load_bias;
4042 if (DataBufferSP data_sp = ReadMemory(process_sp, data_addr, H.p_memsz))
4043 return DataExtractor(data_sp, GetByteOrder(), GetAddressByteSize());
4044 }
4045 }
4046 return DataExtractor();
4047}
4048
4049bool ObjectFileELF::AnySegmentHasPhysicalAddress() {
4050 for (const ELFProgramHeader &H : ProgramHeaders()) {
4051 if (H.p_paddr != 0)
4052 return true;
4053 }
4054 return false;
4055}
4056
4057std::vector<ObjectFile::LoadableData>
4058ObjectFileELF::GetLoadableData(Target &target) {
4059 // Create a list of loadable data from loadable segments, using physical
4060 // addresses if they aren't all null
4061 std::vector<LoadableData> loadables;
4062 bool should_use_paddr = AnySegmentHasPhysicalAddress();
4063 for (const ELFProgramHeader &H : ProgramHeaders()) {
4064 LoadableData loadable;
4065 if (H.p_type != llvm::ELF::PT_LOAD)
4066 continue;
4067 loadable.Dest = should_use_paddr ? H.p_paddr : H.p_vaddr;
4068 if (loadable.Dest == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
4069 continue;
4070 if (H.p_filesz == 0)
4071 continue;
4072 auto segment_data = GetSegmentData(H);
4073 loadable.Contents = llvm::ArrayRef<uint8_t>(segment_data.GetDataStart(),
4074 segment_data.GetByteSize());
4075 loadables.push_back(loadable);
4076 }
4077 return loadables;
4078}
4079
4080lldb::WritableDataBufferSP
4081ObjectFileELF::MapFileDataWritable(const FileSpec &file, uint64_t Size,
4082 uint64_t Offset) {
4083 return FileSystem::Instance().CreateWritableDataBuffer(file.GetPath(), Size,
4084 Offset);
4085}
4086
4087std::optional<DataExtractor>
4088ObjectFileELF::ReadDataFromDynamic(const ELFDynamic *dyn, uint64_t length,
4089 uint64_t offset) {
4090 // ELFDynamic values contain a "d_ptr" member that will be a load address if
4091 // we have an ELF file read from memory, or it will be a file address if it
4092 // was read from a ELF file. This function will correctly fetch data pointed
4093 // to by the ELFDynamic::d_ptr, or return std::nullopt if the data isn't
4094 // available.
4095 const lldb::addr_t d_ptr_addr = dyn->d_ptr + offset;
4096 if (ProcessSP process_sp = m_process_wp.lock()) {
4097 if (DataBufferSP data_sp = ReadMemory(process_sp, d_ptr_addr, length))
4098 return DataExtractor(data_sp, GetByteOrder(), GetAddressByteSize());
4099 } else {
4100 // We have an ELF file with no section headers or we didn't find the
4101 // .dynamic section. Try and find the .dynstr section.
4102 Address addr;
4103 if (!addr.ResolveAddressUsingFileSections(d_ptr_addr, GetSectionList()))
4104 return std::nullopt;
4105 DataExtractor data;
4106 addr.GetSection()->GetSectionData(data);
4107 return DataExtractor(data, d_ptr_addr - addr.GetSection()->GetFileAddress(),
4108 length);
4109 }
4110 return std::nullopt;
4111}
4112
4113std::optional<DataExtractor> ObjectFileELF::GetDynstrData() {
4114 if (SectionList *section_list = GetSectionList()) {
4115 // Find the SHT_DYNAMIC section.
4116 if (Section *dynamic =
4117 section_list
4118 ->FindSectionByType(eSectionTypeELFDynamicLinkInfo, true)
4119 .get()) {
4120 assert(dynamic->GetObjectFile() == this);
4121 if (const ELFSectionHeaderInfo *header =
4122 GetSectionHeaderByIndex(dynamic->GetID())) {
4123 // sh_link: section header index of string table used by entries in
4124 // the section.
4125 if (Section *dynstr =
4126 section_list->FindSectionByID(header->sh_link).get()) {
4127 DataExtractor data;
4128 if (ReadSectionData(dynstr, data))
4129 return data;
4130 }
4131 }
4132 }
4133 }
4134
4135 // Every ELF file which represents an executable or shared library has
4136 // mandatory .dynamic entries. Two of these values are DT_STRTAB and DT_STRSZ
4137 // and represent the dynamic symbol tables's string table. These are needed
4138 // by the dynamic loader and we can read them from a process' address space.
4139 //
4140 // When loading and ELF file from memory, only the program headers are
4141 // guaranteed end up being mapped into memory, and we can find these values in
4142 // the PT_DYNAMIC segment.
4143 const ELFDynamic *strtab = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_STRTAB);
4144 const ELFDynamic *strsz = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_STRSZ);
4145 if (strtab == nullptr || strsz == nullptr)
4146 return std::nullopt;
4147
4148 return ReadDataFromDynamic(strtab, strsz->d_val, /*offset=*/0);
4149}
4150
4151std::optional<lldb_private::DataExtractor> ObjectFileELF::GetDynamicData() {
4152 DataExtractor data;
4153 // The PT_DYNAMIC program header describes where the .dynamic section is and
4154 // doesn't require parsing section headers. The PT_DYNAMIC is required by
4155 // executables and shared libraries so it will always be available.
4156 for (const ELFProgramHeader &H : ProgramHeaders()) {
4157 if (H.p_type == llvm::ELF::PT_DYNAMIC) {
4158 data = GetSegmentData(H);
4159 if (data.GetByteSize() > 0) {
4160 m_dynamic_base_addr = H.p_vaddr;
4161 return data;
4162 }
4163 }
4164 }
4165 // Fall back to using section headers.
4166 if (SectionList *section_list = GetSectionList()) {
4167 // Find the SHT_DYNAMIC section.
4168 if (Section *dynamic =
4169 section_list
4170 ->FindSectionByType(eSectionTypeELFDynamicLinkInfo, true)
4171 .get()) {
4172 assert(dynamic->GetObjectFile() == this);
4173 if (ReadSectionData(dynamic, data)) {
4174 m_dynamic_base_addr = dynamic->GetFileAddress();
4175 return data;
4176 }
4177 }
4178 }
4179 return std::nullopt;
4180}
4181
4182std::optional<uint32_t> ObjectFileELF::GetNumSymbolsFromDynamicHash() {
4183 const ELFDynamic *hash = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_HASH);
4184 if (hash == nullptr)
4185 return std::nullopt;
4186
4187 // The DT_HASH header looks like this:
4188 struct DtHashHeader {
4189 uint32_t nbucket;
4190 uint32_t nchain;
4191 };
4192 if (auto data = ReadDataFromDynamic(hash, 8)) {
4193 // We don't need the number of buckets value "nbucket", we just need the
4194 // "nchain" value which contains the number of symbols.
4195 offset_t offset = offsetof(DtHashHeader, nchain);
4196 return data->GetU32(&offset);
4197 }
4198
4199 return std::nullopt;
4200}
4201
4202std::optional<uint32_t> ObjectFileELF::GetNumSymbolsFromDynamicGnuHash() {
4203 const ELFDynamic *gnu_hash = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_GNU_HASH);
4204 if (gnu_hash == nullptr)
4205 return std::nullopt;
4206
4207 // Create a DT_GNU_HASH header
4208 // https://flapenguin.me/elf-dt-gnu-hash
4209 struct DtGnuHashHeader {
4210 uint32_t nbuckets = 0;
4211 uint32_t symoffset = 0;
4212 uint32_t bloom_size = 0;
4213 uint32_t bloom_shift = 0;
4214 };
4215 uint32_t num_symbols = 0;
4216 // Read enogh data for the DT_GNU_HASH header so we can extract the values.
4217 if (auto data = ReadDataFromDynamic(gnu_hash, sizeof(DtGnuHashHeader))) {
4218 offset_t offset = 0;
4219 DtGnuHashHeader header;
4220 header.nbuckets = data->GetU32(&offset);
4221 header.symoffset = data->GetU32(&offset);
4222 header.bloom_size = data->GetU32(&offset);
4223 header.bloom_shift = data->GetU32(&offset);
4224 const size_t addr_size = GetAddressByteSize();
4225 const addr_t buckets_offset =
4226 sizeof(DtGnuHashHeader) + addr_size * header.bloom_size;
4227 std::vector<uint32_t> buckets;
4228 if (auto bucket_data = ReadDataFromDynamic(gnu_hash, header.nbuckets * 4,
4229 buckets_offset)) {
4230 offset = 0;
4231 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < header.nbuckets; ++i)
4232 buckets.push_back(bucket_data->GetU32(&offset));
4233 // Locate the chain that handles the largest index bucket.
4234 uint32_t last_symbol = 0;
4235 for (uint32_t bucket_value : buckets)
4236 last_symbol = std::max(bucket_value, last_symbol);
4237 if (last_symbol < header.symoffset) {
4238 num_symbols = header.symoffset;
4239 } else {
4240 // Walk the bucket's chain to add the chain length to the total.
4241 const addr_t chains_base_offset = buckets_offset + header.nbuckets * 4;
4242 for (;;) {
4243 if (auto chain_entry_data = ReadDataFromDynamic(
4244 gnu_hash, 4,
4245 chains_base_offset + (last_symbol - header.symoffset) * 4)) {
4246 offset = 0;
4247 uint32_t chain_entry = chain_entry_data->GetU32(&offset);
4248 ++last_symbol;
4249 // If the low bit is set, this entry is the end of the chain.
4250 if (chain_entry & 1)
4251 break;
4252 } else {
4253 break;
4254 }
4255 }
4256 num_symbols = last_symbol;
4257 }
4258 }
4259 }
4260 if (num_symbols > 0)
4261 return num_symbols;
4262
4263 return std::nullopt;
4264}
4265
4266std::optional<DataExtractor>
4267ObjectFileELF::GetDynsymDataFromDynamic(uint32_t &num_symbols) {
4268 // Every ELF file which represents an executable or shared library has
4269 // mandatory .dynamic entries. The DT_SYMTAB value contains a pointer to the
4270 // symbol table, and DT_SYMENT contains the size of a symbol table entry.
4271 // We then can use either the DT_HASH or DT_GNU_HASH to find the number of
4272 // symbols in the symbol table as the symbol count is not stored in the
4273 // .dynamic section as a key/value pair.
4274 //
4275 // When loading and ELF file from memory, only the program headers end up
4276 // being mapped into memory, and we can find these values in the PT_DYNAMIC
4277 // segment.
4278 num_symbols = 0;
4279 // Get the process in case this is an in memory ELF file.
4280 ProcessSP process_sp(m_process_wp.lock());
4281 const ELFDynamic *symtab = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_SYMTAB);
4282 const ELFDynamic *syment = FindDynamicSymbol(DT_SYMENT);
4283 // DT_SYMTAB and DT_SYMENT are mandatory.
4284 if (symtab == nullptr || syment == nullptr)
4285 return std::nullopt;
4286
4287 if (std::optional<uint32_t> syms = GetNumSymbolsFromDynamicHash())
4288 num_symbols = *syms;
4289 else if (std::optional<uint32_t> syms = GetNumSymbolsFromDynamicGnuHash())
4290 num_symbols = *syms;
4291 else
4292 return std::nullopt;
4293 if (num_symbols == 0)
4294 return std::nullopt;
4295 return ReadDataFromDynamic(symtab, syment->d_val * num_symbols);
4296}
